info.title: Agent Swarm API
info.description:
Multi-agent orchestration API for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI. Enables task distribution, agent communication, and service discovery.

MCP tools are documented separately in [MCP.md](./MCP.md).

get.summary: List active sessions

delete.summary: Delete active session by task ID

put.summary: Update heartbeat for an active session

get.summary: List all agents
Fetch agent + global setup scripts for Docker entrypoint

put.summary: Update agent profile (role, description, capabilities, etc.)

get.summary: Get a single agent
Get resolved config (merged global + agent + repo scopes)
Get a single config entry by ID
List config entries with optional filters

put.summary: Create or update a config entry

get.summary: Get PM2 ecosystem config for agent services
List epics with optional filters
Get epic with progress and tasks

post.summary: Add task to epic (create new or assign existing)

get.summary: List all channels
Get messages in a channel

post.summary: Post a message to a channel

get.summary: Get thread messages for a message

post.summary: Ingest content into memory system (async embedding)
Search memories by natural language query

get.summary: Resolve a prompt template for a given event type and scope chain
List all registered event types with their available variables

post.summary: Dry-run render a template with provided variables
Full scope-aware template resolution with interpolation (used by workers via HTTP)
Checkout a specific version of a prompt template from history
Reset a prompt template to its code-defined default

404.description: Template not found or no code default available

get.summary: Get a single prompt template with its version history
List prompt templates with optional filters

put.summary: Create or update a prompt template override

get.summary: Poll for triggers (tasks, mentions, epic updates)
Get a repo by ID
List repos with optional filters
Get a schedule by ID
Get session logs for a task
Query session costs with filters
Aggregated session cost summary
Cost today and month-to-date for dashboard
List agent logs
Dashboard summary stats
List all registered services
List scheduled tasks
Get concurrent session context for lead awareness
List tasks with filters

put.summary: Update Claude session ID for a task

post.summary: Cancel a pending or in-progress task

get.summary: Get task details with logs

post.summary: Mark task as completed or failed (runner endpoint)

get.summary: Get paused tasks for this agent
Redirect to Linear OAuth consent screen
Handle Linear OAuth callback
Linear connection status, token expiry, workspace info, expected webhook URL
List all workflows

id.description: Unique node identifier, used in 'next' and 'inputs' mappings

config.description: Executor-specific config. For agent-task: { template, outputSchema?, agentId?, tags?, priority?, dir?, vcsRepo?, model? }. Values support {{interpolation}} from the node's inputs context. NOTE: config.outputSchema on agent-task nodes validates the AGENT's raw JSON output, while node-level outputSchema validates the EXECUTOR's return value ({taskId, taskOutput}).

next.description: Next node(s): string for simple chaining, or record for port-based routing ({pass: 'a', fail: 'b'})

inputs.description: REQUIRED for cross-node data access. Maps local names to context paths. Without this, upstream step outputs are NOT available for interpolation — only 'trigger' and 'input' are. Example: { "cityData": "generate-city" } → use {{cityData.taskOutput.field}} in config templates. For trigger data: { "pr": "trigger.pullRequest" }.

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate resolved inputs before execution

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate the executor's output (e.g. {taskId, taskOutput} for agent-task). Different from config.outputSchema which validates the agent's raw output.

get.summary: Get a workflow by ID

id.description: Unique node identifier, used in 'next' and 'inputs' mappings

config.description: Executor-specific config. For agent-task: { template, outputSchema?, agentId?, tags?, priority?, dir?, vcsRepo?, model? }. Values support {{interpolation}} from the node's inputs context. NOTE: config.outputSchema on agent-task nodes validates the AGENT's raw JSON output, while node-level outputSchema validates the EXECUTOR's return value ({taskId, taskOutput}).

next.description: Next node(s): string for simple chaining, or record for port-based routing ({pass: 'a', fail: 'b'})

inputs.description: REQUIRED for cross-node data access. Maps local names to context paths. Without this, upstream step outputs are NOT available for interpolation — only 'trigger' and 'input' are. Example: { "cityData": "generate-city" } → use {{cityData.taskOutput.field}} in config templates. For trigger data: { "pr": "trigger.pullRequest" }.

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate resolved inputs before execution

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema to validate the executor's output (e.g. {taskId, taskOutput} for agent-task). Different from config.outputSchema which validates the agent's raw output.

201.description: Workflow run started (or skipped if cooldown active)

get.summary: List runs for a workflow
Get a workflow run with steps (includes retry columns)

200.description: Workflow run details with steps including retry info

get.summary: List all executor types with their config and output schemas

200.description: List of executor types with schemas

get.summary: Get a specific executor type with its schemas
List version history for a workflow
Get a specific version snapshot of a workflow
